title: 9 students brought to hospital after school bus crash in Everett
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Nine of 11 students were taken to hospitals for observation after a school bus crash in Everett.
The crash occurred at Broadway and Langton Street involving a school bus and a black car.
The cause of the accident is under investigation; no serious injuries were reported.

title: Huge endangered snapping turtle, somehow loose in Mass., rescued from pond
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: An endangered alligator snapping turtle was rescued from an Essex County pond in Massachusetts by wildlife officials.
The turtle, a non-native species suffering from pneumonia, was likely abandoned by a pet owner, authorities said.
The reptile was taken to New England Wildlife Center for recovery and may be returned to its native habitat.

title: After state trooper killed, Senate approves amendment aimed at deterring wrong-way drivers
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Massachusetts Senate votes 40-0 to mandate infrastructure upgrades to deter wrong-way drivers after a fatal crash.
Trooper Kevin Trainor was killed by a wrong-way driver in Lynnfield; the Senate responds with safety measures.
The amendment mandates MassDOT to implement systems like signage and alerts to prevent wrong-way driving incidents.

title: Thoughts on owning one gas and one electric vehicle
author: John Paul
bullets: John Paul advises a mixed garage with one gas and one electric vehicle for diverse driving needs.
Home charging solutions, like the MSI Smart Charger, are highlighted as crucial for electric vehicle practicality.
Public charging infrastructure is expanding, making long-distance EV travel more feasible over time.

title: Red Sox top prospect Franklin Arias makes major leap in latest ESPN rankings
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Franklin Arias, a Red Sox prospect, rose from No. 36 to No. 6 in ESPN's latest rankings.
Arias is batting .328 with eight homers and 25 RBIs in 33 games with Double-A Portland.
Signed in 2023, Arias is praised for defense and improved offense, poised for potential All-Star status.

title: Police respond to threat made against Perkins School for the Blind
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Watertown police responded to a threat against Perkins School for the Blind, causing a lockdown Thursday morning.
The situation at Perkins School was resolved without danger, and police will not file criminal charges.
Watertown police cited student privacy as the reason for not releasing further information about the incident.

title: Second arrest made in alleged sexual abuse of dog in Connecticut
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Damon Mesite, 28, arrested in Meriden, Connecticut, charged with aiding and abetting sexual assault of an animal.
Kristiina Kennedy, 45, previously charged in April for sexually assaulting her dog and producing pornography.
Authorities seized pets and devices; Mesite's link to Kennedy remains undisclosed.

title: Bruins reportedly moving on from one of their assistant coaches
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Bruins are not renewing assistant coach Jay Leach's contract for the upcoming season.
Jay Leach rejoined the Bruins in June 2024, focusing on defense and penalty kill strategies.
Despite improvements in individual player performance, the Bruins' defense ranked poorly under Leach's guidance.

title: NFL insider would be ‘surprised’ if Patriots don’t land A.J. Brown
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: NFL insider Ian Rapoport expects the Patriots to pursue A.J. Brown, but no deal is imminent.
Brown's cap hit could be spread over two years starting June 1, potentially influencing trade discussions.
Speculation includes Kayshon Boutte possibly being part of the trade to acquire A.J. Brown.

title: Here’s how much a last-minute trip to Cape Cod for Memorial Day weekend costs
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: A day trip to Cape Cod, including gas, meals, and activities, totals approximately $162.75.
A couple's getaway from Friday to Monday on Cape Cod is estimated at $1,867.55, factoring in lodging and expenses.
A family vacation for four on Cape Cod, with accommodations and activities, costs around $2,331.35 for Memorial Day weekend.
